comments.dark: Strong variables are mostly used for hover states, in dark mode they invert and become lighter. They fail 4.5:1 contrast with labels, but we only need the default state to pass.

comments.light: The Run button orange is an intentional a11y exception. It fails 4.5:1 contrast with white labels but we are keeping it for its deep user association with "run" (and Blockly's run blocks).
Bumped to level 70 (brand-guideline primary is 50) so white labels pass 4.5:1; strong sits at 90.
comments.dark: Level 70 is preserved for success even in dark mode to continue to pass 4.5:1 contrast with white labels, we intentionally don't invert to avoid black labels.
Bc success is at level 70, we maintain the relative hover rule by setting the strong to 60.

comments.light: Bumped to level 70 (brand-guideline primary is 50) so white labels pass 4.5:1; strong sits at 90.
comments.dark: Level 70 is preserved for info even in dark mode to continue to pass 4.5:1 contrast with white labels, we intentionally don't invert to avoid black labels.
Bc info is at level 70, we maintain the relative hover rule by setting the strong to 60.
Primary borders of all colors stay at level 50 in dark mode since contrast only needs 3:1.

comments.light: Stays at level 50 (unlike the bumped background primary at 70) since border contrast only needs 3:1.
Stays at level 50 (unlike the bumped background primary at 70) since border contrast only needs 3:1.
Orange text intentionally keeps the true brand-guideline level-50 value instead of bumping for contrast
Selected state is mode-inverted and cross-family by design: brand fill + seafoam text in light, seafoam fill + navy text in dark.
comments.dark: Selected state is mode-inverted and cross-family by design: brand fill + seafoam text in light, seafoam fill + navy text in dark.

comments.light: Selected state text pairs with background and border-state-selected — always use the two together.
comments.dark: Pairs with background-state-selected — always use the two together.

comments.light: Border state selected strong and primary intentionally match the background state color.
Matches background-neutral-quaternary by value today, but disabled intent is decoupled so the state can be restyled independently.

Single inverse for the whole disabled family: label color for solid-fill disabled elements of any color; pair with the matching background/disabled/<color>. Outline disabled elements use text/disabled/<color> instead.
